name: Urban Combat: Jackers (Mixed Reality FPS campaign)

reviews.review_description:
The way this game blends full VR with mixed reality is something I’ve never seen before. You’re fully inside a virtual world, moving around freely, aiming, crouching, turning with your body but at the same time, you still see part of your real room, especially the lower area. That means you can move naturally without worrying about bumping into anything. It feels like true VR immersion without losing awareness of your space.

What really impressed me is how the world behaves. As you move forward, objects don’t stay “inside” a virtual screen or portal—they actually come out into your real space. Enemies, bullets, even large objects—everything transitions into your room as you approach. That alone makes the gameplay feel completely different from other MR games.

Gameplay-wise, it feels like a mix of Call of Duty and Star Wars. There’s a lot of variety, and I’ve only played the first six missions (basically the intro), and even that had a surprising amount of different scenarios—like fighting and destroying a giant robot that literally steps into your room. That moment alone sold it for me.
I’ll be honest—I was very unsure about downloading this game at first. But after playing it, I can say it’s one of the most original and unique VR experiences I’ve tried on Quest 3. 

A Feedback for the developers:

You have something special here—a genuinely unique game—but I think the marketing is holding it back.
From a player’s perspective: when I first saw the store page with only images and no real gameplay video, my first thought was that the game might not be finished or might not be real. That alone almost stopped me from downloading it.

Yes, there are videos on YouTube, but they focus more on explaining the technology rather than showing real gameplay. And honestly, what you show there does not reflect how good the actual gameplay feels.
If I had judged the game only by the current marketing, I would not have bought it.
You need:

A real gameplay video on the Meta Quest Store page
Clear footage showing both open and closed room setups
A better representation of what makes this game different
Right now, it looks like “just another MR FPS,” when in reality it’s something much more unique.

What I mean is This game has huge potential. The gameplay itself is already there—it sells itself once you try it.
The problem isn’t the game. It’s how it’s presented.
Fix that, and a lot more players will give it a chance.

It's really fun and interesting! This game is still in the works, and it's a blast watching it grow, and being part of it is pretty wild. It's the first game I have ever seen implementing so many options on the way you play.  Play on VR headsets or an app on your phone. Play single player or multiple players at once. Play in a small space sitting in a swivel chair or in a large area to run around in.  Join the Discord to follow further development and get some back an forth with the friendly developers that are eager to listen to your feedback.

This mixed reality game merges outdoor activity with FPS gameplay, offering an innovative and engaging experience. It's a great choice for players looking to add some physicality to their gaming sessions. Worth checking out!
